summary:
• Escalating U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions and deepening Strait of Hormuz crisis are driving oil price volatility and creating acute supply uncertainty for Western Hemisphere refiners dependent on Middle Eastern crude flows.

• OPEC output has collapsed to 26-year lows amid Iran war impacts while Chinese refinery margins are crushed by the Hormuz supply disruption, reducing global demand for Americas crude exports and weakening price support.

• Nigeria's refinery rehabilitation failures are forcing reliance on Chinese firms, signaling a structural shift in refining capacity dynamics that could redirect crude flows away from traditional Western Hemisphere trading partners.
